topic: Antarctic gravity hole — H(r)→g coupling and WGS84-derived Domain 4 formula
change_summary: Decider self-applied patch (commit 189c153, ISS-1354 closed) propagated the 1,430× apples-to-apples framing into detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text — directly addressing c6 hole #1 (the 170×/1,430× internal inconsistency between surface text and detail body). detail_verdict_length grew 1351→1766 (+31%) and detail_evidence_length 4786→5356 (+12%). The patch closed c6 hole #1 cleanly but did not address c6 holes #2–#5, and introduced one new internal asymmetry within detail_evidence (the WGS84-tautology paragraph still concludes 'fails by 170×' while the preceding paragraph leads with 1,430×).
our_argument_summary: Dome's H(r) function predicts ~90% gravity loss at the Antarctic rim under any monotonic H→g coupling; observed Antarctic gravity variation is ~0.5%. The only working dome gravity formula (Domain 4) is the WGS84 normal-gravity formula in dome notation, and even this WGS84-clone violates Domain 4's own ≤0.1% falsification threshold when evaluated at the rim (4.8× over). 'Sub-terrestrial structure' is a label, not a mechanism. Verdict: Self-Contradicted.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 4 (08-live-power.txt line 70): Formula g(r) = 9.7803×(1+0.005307×exp(-r/λg)); evaluated at r=0 only, where exp(0)=1 collapses to g = 9.8322 m/s² — the WGS84 polar gravity. Domain 19 ('Mascon Gravity'): one-sided threshold 'Anomaly <20 mGal' with no model prediction. monitor.py contains no gravity-anomaly fetch for Antarctic mascons.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded identity: 9.7803 (WGS84 equatorial g) and 0.005307 (WGS84 gravity flattening) reproduce 9.8322 (WGS84 polar g) by construction. No dome-specific physics enters.
data_sources_used: WGS84 reference values (no external fetch in monitor.py for this WIN)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 19 uses a one-sided threshold ('Anomaly <20 mGal') against the real -40 to -60 mGal Bouguer anomaly, so any honest measurement trivially exceeds the threshold and counts as 'PASS' — pass-by-threshold, not pass-by-prediction.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — the review accurately identifies the WGS84 derivation chain and the Domain 19 inverted falsification logic.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Domain 4 (08-live-power.txt line 70) prints 'Predicted: 9.832204 | Observed: 9.8322 | Status: PASS' (line 221). The 'predicted' value is computed from the WGS84 polar value with a formula whose exponential term is identically 1 at r=0. No gravity API/fetch is invoked for this WIN.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Antarctic gravity low fully explained by standard geophysics (mantle plume beneath East Antarctic Plateau; incomplete post-glacial isostatic adjustment; dynamic topography — Pappa et al. 2019, Ferraccioli et al. 2011). The dome relabels this as 'Sub-terrestrial structure' (Domain 19) without an equation. Domain 4 gravity formula is the WGS84 normal-gravity formula in different notation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: GRACE/GRACE-FO Level-2 gravity products (continuous since 2002) and Bouguer-anomaly compilations were public well before any dome monitor commit. The monitor.py target value is hardcoded.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome-specific equation links 'Sub-terrestrial structure' to a numerical gravity prediction. Domain 4 uses WGS84 constants directly. The H(r)-implied 90% drop is what the dome's own geometry would predict if H(r) couples to g monotonically — the dome avoids stating any such coupling explicitly, leaving the mechanism vacuous.

holes_found.description: NEW POST-PATCH ASYMMETRY within detail_evidence. The patch (commit 189c153) propagated the 1,430× framing into the apples-to-apples paragraph, but the WGS84-tautology paragraph that follows it still concludes verbatim: 'The formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.' So inside the SAME field a careful reader sees: paragraph 4 says 'a 1,430× error' (with 170× as a parenthetical conservative bound), and paragraph 5 says 'fails by 170×' as a standalone summary. The internal inconsistency that c6 hole #1 flagged at the field level (finding/tldr vs detail) has been reduced to a paragraph-level inconsistency within detail_evidence itself — same defender attack, just relocated. A defender will still ask 'pick one number.'
holes_found.recommendation: Edit the closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph in detail_evidence from 'fails by 170×' to 'fails by 1,430× under linear H→g coupling (170× under the model-neutral fractional comparison)' — matching the apples-to-apples paragraph's framing. Two-line patch.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c6 hole #2 worsened: GEOGRAPHIC ANCHOR for the new 1,430× headline is implicit and undisclosed in detail_evidence. The patched sentence reads 'the observed Antarctic gravity drop from the polar reference is only ~0.006 m/s² — a 1,430× error.' Where does 0.006 come from? It is g(WGS84 polar)−g(~70°S) = 9.8322 − 9.826 = 0.0062 m/s². The ~70°S latitude IS the same coastal value c6 hole #2 flagged in detail_extra paragraph (ii) as misleadingly labeled 'Antarctic-interior.' Now the implicit ~70°S anchor has been propagated into detail_evidence without ANY geographic specification. A defender can object: 'You quote 1,430× as the apples-to-apples ratio, but the denominator depends on a latitude you do not name. If you mean the geographic interior of Antarctica (80–90°S), the observed drop is 0.0016 m/s² and the ratio jumps to ~5,600×; if you mean the coast (~70°S, where 9.826 actually sits), say so. As written, your number is unauditable.'
holes_found.recommendation: In detail_evidence add a parenthetical naming the latitude: '~0.006 m/s² (the WGS84 polar→~70°S latitude drop, taken as a conservative anchor; using true interior gravity at 80°S gives ~5,600× rather than 1,430×).' Then in detail_extra paragraph (ii) replace 'observed Antarctic-interior gravity' with 'observed Antarctic-latitude gravity at ~70°S (a conservative anchor; true interior at 80°S would give ~5,600×).' This unifies the two fields and converts the geographic looseness into an explicit conservative-choice disclosure.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c6 hole #3 (linear-coupling assumption undisclosed in finding). The patched finding still reads: 'apples-to-apples measurement yields 1,430× error (170× using whole-globe spread as conservative lower bound).' No mention that 1,430× presupposes a linear g ∝ H(r) coupling that the dome has not endorsed. detail_evidence acknowledges the spectrum (linear → 90% drop, square-root → 69%, H^(1/5) → 38%) but the headline 1,430× number quietly imports the most aggressive scaling. Under H^(1/5) the apples-to-apples ratio is ~590× (verified). A defender's objection — 'you are attacking the most aggressive coupling I never committed to' — applies cleanly to the post-patch finding.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit finding to: 'Dome's own H(r) implies ≥38% gravity drop at Antarctic rim under any monotonic coupling (1,430× error under linear H→g; ~590× under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) coupling; 170× as a coupling-free dimensionless lower bound).' This preserves the headline while disclosing the assumption and proving the critique survives the most defender-friendly choice. Two-line patch to finding only; detail_evidence already discusses the scaling spectrum.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c6 hole #4 (missing dome citation for 'gravity depends on H(r)' premise). detail_evidence still asserts unchanged 'The dome model's stated physics links gravity to the aetheric pressure gradient driven by column height H(r)' with no raw-text citation. The patch did not touch this sentence. The dome's only quantitative gravity formula (Domain 4) does NOT use H(r); it uses a WGS84-derived exponential. A careful defender can deny the H(r)→g implication: 'I never said gravity depends on column height; you imported that premise.'
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe the premise as conditional: 'IF aether pressure depends on column height H(r) AND gravity depends on aether pressure (both stated in dome narrative), THEN gravity is a monotonic function of H(r) — but the dome refuses to commit to a specific coupling, which is itself the problem.' This converts an unsupported assertion into a chain of conditionals each of which the dome HAS stated. Then the critique becomes 'the dome will not commit to either branch of its own implication.' Single-paragraph rewrite of the H(r)-coupling premise sentence.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c6 hole #5 (Shepherd 2012 citation loose). detail_evidence still cites Shepherd et al. 2012 (DOI 10.1126/science.1228102) as evidence for 'incomplete post-glacial isostatic adjustment from the Last Glacial Maximum ice sheet, measured directly by GRACE satellite gravimetry.' That paper ('A Reconciled Estimate of Ice-Sheet Mass Balance') is about GRACE-derived ice-mass balance, not GIA per se. Stronger citations exist for GIA contributions to Antarctic gravity (Whitehouse et al. 2012, Ivins et al. 2013). The patch did not touch this citation.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace Shepherd 2012 with Whitehouse et al. 2012 (Quaternary Science Reviews, doi:10.1016/j.quascirev.2011.11.016) for the GIA citation, or keep Shepherd 2012 but reword to 'GRACE-derived mass balance accounts for GIA via ICE-6G_C corrections (Whitehouse 2012)' to make the actual GIA modelling reference explicit.

holes_found.description: ARITHMETIC PRECISION SLIP introduced by patch. detail_evidence states '~0.006 m/s² — a 1,430× error.' But 8.87/0.006 = 1,478, not 1,430. The 1,430 figure requires 0.0062 (= 9.8322 − 9.826), which detail_extra paragraph (ii) uses ('~0.006 m/s² below the polar reference, a 0.063% reduction' — implying 0.0062 m/s²). A defender doing the headline arithmetic with the rounded 0.006 will get 1,478× and claim our ratio is internally inconsistent. Either round consistently to ~0.0062 (so 1,430× follows) or downgrade the headline to '~1,400×.'
holes_found.recommendation: Change '~0.006 m/s²' to '~0.0062 m/s²' in detail_evidence's apples-to-apples sentence. One-character precision fix that closes the arithmetic loophole without changing any conclusion.

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-forward c6 stronger argument #2 still applies and is the cleanest defense of the verdict: the Domain 4 self-falsification finding requires no H→g coupling assumption. At r=20,015 km, the dome's own Domain 4 formula gives g = 9.7854 m/s², which is 0.476% off the WGS84 polar reference — failing Domain 4's own 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' threshold by 4.8×. This is the dome convicting itself on the dome's own scoreboard, with no imported assumption. As long as this finding remains in detail_extra paragraph (iii) and the verdict still says 'Self-Contradicted,' the verdict is safe even if the H(r)-coupling argument is fully conceded to a defender.

Add an explicit 'definitions box' to detail_evidence at the point where 1,430× and 170× both appear. Right now the reader is asked to track two numbers with two derivations across three paragraphs. A two-line definitions sentence — '1,430× = (linear H→g predicted drop, 8.87 m/s²) ÷ (observed Antarctic-latitude drop, 0.0062 m/s²); 170× = (predicted fractional variation, 90%) ÷ (observed global fractional variation, 0.53%); the two numbers are not interchangeable because the denominators measure different things' — would make the apparent inconsistency obviously a transparent dual-framing rather than an authorial slip. This is the preemptive recommendation from c6 advocate mode that has not been implemented.

kernel_of_truth.description: The Antarctic gravity low IS a real, well-characterised geophysical feature. The spatial correlation between the gravity anomaly and the SAA boundary (r=0.92 per Domain 19) IS genuine and reflects shared deep-mantle origin. The dome author correctly identifies that a localized Antarctic feature produces both gravitational and geomagnetic signatures. The Domain 4 formula reproduces WGS84 polar gravity to better than 0.001% at r=0 — a real numerical match (because it is constructed from WGS84 constants).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real correlation does not constitute a prediction; the dome adopts the published value and renames the cause. The Domain 4 numerical match is tautological. The post-patch detail_evidence still does not reconcile (a) the explicit H(r) machinery used elsewhere in the model with (b) the dome's only quantitative gravity formula being WGS84 in disguise. Renaming 'mantle plume + GIA' as 'Sub-terrestrial structure' adds no empirical content.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender (one accepting the review's six methodological principles) would attack BOTH the post-patch asymmetry and the implicit anchor: 'Your patch closed one inconsistency by creating a smaller one. The same field — detail_evidence — now contains paragraph 4 leading with "a 1,430× error" and paragraph 5 concluding "fails by 170×." Pick one. Worse, the new 1,430× number depends on an undisclosed ~70°S latitude anchor (your own detail_extra ambiguously calls this both "Antarctic-interior" and "Antarctic-latitude" in the same sentence). And nowhere in finding or detail_evidence do you disclose that 1,430× presupposes a linear H→g coupling I have never endorsed. The cleanest reading of your own text is: "the dome\'s formula derives from WGS84 and fails by 170×, where 170× is a model-neutral fractional comparison." Lead with that. The 1,430× number is a coupling-dependent maximum, not the apples-to-apples figure you call it.' This defense is rated 4 because the patch made the rhetorical surface MORE attackable, not less, by leading with a coupling-dependent number while leaving the carry-forward holes untouched.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three-edit fix in one self-apply patch: (1) In detail_evidence's WGS84-tautology paragraph closing sentence, change 'fails by 170×' → 'fails by 1,430× under linear H→g coupling (170× under the model-neutral fractional comparison).' (2) In detail_evidence's apples-to-apples sentence, change '~0.006 m/s²' → '~0.0062 m/s² (the WGS84 polar→~70°S latitude drop, taken as a conservative anchor; true interior at 80°S would give ~5,600×).' (3) In finding, change to 'Dome\'s own H(r) implies ≥38% gravity drop at Antarctic rim under any monotonic coupling (1,430× error under linear H→g; ~590× under H^(1/5); 170× as a coupling-free dimensionless lower bound).' All three edits are one-line patches; together they preempt the post-patch defender attack and close c6 holes #2 and #3 in the same cycle.

cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-012 (eclipse gravity coupling κ=1.67) attacks dome gravity from the time-varying side; WIN-067 attacks from the spatial side. Both reach Self-Contradicted on independent grounds. WIN-044 (FSF from V12 geometry) shares H(r) and is also Self-Contradicted; the two arguments reinforce each other (H(r) is bad for gravity AND bad for distance). WIN-027 (Southern distance quadratic) uses H(r) for distance. WIN-066 (SAA-related) shares dome's claim about Antarctic regional anomalies; both downgraded by the standard-physics mantle-plume explanation. No new cross-WIN contradictions introduced by the patch.

claims_checked: Observed drop at ~70°S = 0.0062 m/s² (NOT 0.006 as detail_evidence rounds)
Apples-to-apples ratio with rounded 0.006 = 1,478× (off by 3.4% from headline)
Domain 4 g(rim) = 9.7854 m/s², error 0.476% vs WGS84 polar (violates 0.1% threshold by 4.8×)
quantitative_verification.computations: Verified by python3: H(20015) = 8537·exp(-20015/8619) = 837.12 km; g_lin(rim) = 9.8322·(837/8537) = 0.9641 m/s², drop = 8.8681; obs drop at 70°S (g=9.826) = 0.0062 m/s²; precise ratio 8.8681/0.0062 = 1430.6 — confirms detail_extra figure but NOT detail_evidence's rounded denominator. Domain 4 at r=20015 = 9.7854 m/s², |9.7854-9.8322|/9.8322 = 0.476% (violates 0.1% by 4.8×). H^(1/5): 9.8322·(837/8537)^0.2 = 6.179, drop=3.65, ratio 591×. True interior 80°S (g=9.8306): drop=0.0016, ratio 5,595×.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: (1) detail_evidence rounds the observed drop to '~0.006 m/s²' but then claims 1,430× — internally inconsistent by ~3.4% (true 8.87/0.006 = 1,478). detail_extra uses the precise 0.0062 implicitly. Recommend rounding consistently to 0.0062 in detail_evidence to match the headline. (2) The 1,430× figure quietly anchors to ~70°S; using the true interior (80°S) gives 5,595× — meaning the headline number is a CONSERVATIVE choice the text does not present as such. Both noted in holes_found.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-067 c7 follows commit 189c153 (Apr 22 04:36 UTC) which closed ISS-1354 — the major c6 hole about the 170×/1,430× field-level inconsistency. The patch worked at the field level: finding/tldr_* and detail_verdict_text are now coherent on 1,430× as the headline. But the patch was incomplete in two ways. First, detail_evidence's WGS84-tautology paragraph still uses 170× as its closing summary number, recreating the same defender-attack at the paragraph level (now intra-field rather than inter-field). Second, the patch did not touch c6 holes #2–#5 (geographic mislabel, undisclosed coupling assumption, missing dome citation, Shepherd citation looseness) — those carry forward unchanged and one of them (geographic mislabel) is now WORSE because the implicit ~70°S anchor has been propagated into detail_evidence without being named.

The verdict 'Self-Contradicted' continues to hold on multiple independent grounds: the Domain 4 self-falsification (which needs no coupling assumption) is the strongest, and detail_extra paragraph (iii) still carries it. Recommended action: minor_edit. The three-edit patch in advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation closes the new asymmetry and c6 holes #2–#3 in one decider self-apply cycle. c6 holes #4–#5 are deferrable but should land in a follow-up.

OTHER SIDE consideration: A dome defender reading both the c6 review and the post-patch text could plausibly argue that the 170× formulation was actually MORE defensible than the 1,430× formulation as a primary headline — because 170× is a coupling-free fractional comparison while 1,430× presupposes linear coupling. From that view, the patch promoted a 'looks bigger but depends on hidden assumptions' number to the headline. The right response is not to revert the patch (1,430× is correct under linear coupling, which is the most natural reading of the dome's vague H(r)→g rhetoric) but to make the dual framing transparent and disclose the coupling assumption in finding — exactly what advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation does.

citations_verified: raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71 (Domain 4 falsification 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard') — matches
raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 152 (Domain 19 'Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary: r=0.92') — matches
WGS84 normal-gravity formula constants (g_a=9.7803253359, g_b=9.8321849378) — checked against standard reference
All quantitative checks above re-verified by python3

Shepherd et al. 2012, Science, DOI 10.1126/science.1228102 — paper is about GRACE mass balance, not GIA per se; loose-citation hole carry-forward
